created_by: Wilderculture WILD Toolbox session (Claude) — woodland group batch; built from field sheet 10 (Native Woodland), split per UKHab type. v3.3.

ukhab_level5_states.w1a5: Western acidic oak woodland (Annex I H91A0) — the Atlantic temperate rainforest oak

ukhab_identity.ukhab_level5_note: Keyed at w1a (Level 4). w1a5 (Western acidic oak woodland, H91A0) is the Annex I rainforest type recorded per parcel.
ukhab_identity.secondary_codes_notes: Temperate rainforest is a concept overlaying w1a (and w1e/hazel), not a separate code — flag via condition/notes.
ukhab_identity.annex1_habitat: H91A0 Old sessile oak woods (the w1a5 type)

sources.title: UK Habitat Classification v2.01 (official definitions + master data sheet + applied surveys)
sources.specific_claim: w = Woodland and forest, w1 = Broadleaved/mixed/yew, w1a = Upland oakwood, recorded as w1a5 Western acidic oak woodland (H91A0). Confirmed in official UKHab Habitat Definitions, master data sheet and applied surveys.

nvc_communities: W11 Quercus petraea–Betula pubescens–Oxalis acetosella woodland
W17 Quercus petraea–Betula pubescens–Dicranum majus woodland (the western/rainforest type)
Atlantic hazelwoods belong with this group
cross_references.nvc_communities_notes: W17 is the moss/liverwort/lichen-rich western rainforest oak; W11 the more eastern upland oak. Ash does not predominate (that is w1b).

sources.title: NVC Field Guide to Woodland (W11, W17) / British Plant Communities
sources.specific_claim: W11/W17 are the upland oak-birch communities; W17 the western rainforest type.

ecology.description: Sessile oak, downy birch, rowan and holly woodland on acid upland slopes — the natural climax of much acid upland ground below the treeline. In the wet oceanic west it becomes Atlantic temperate rainforest, festooned with mosses, liverworts, lichens and filmy ferns — internationally important and restricted to Britain and Ireland's western seaboard. Not primarily a grazing resource: the question is almost never how to stock it but how to get the browsing level right so it can regenerate and persist.
condition_indicators_good: Full range of tree ages — seedlings, saplings, building and mature trees, old veterans, standing and fallen deadwood (regeneration is the diagnostic)
Rich, intact ground flora; in the west, abundant mosses, lichens and ferns
Native species dominant; open glades and a graded woodland edge
Browsing light enough that regeneration survives — a wood renewing itself
condition_indicators_poor: Overgrazed (the common failure): closed canopy of old trees, no young ones, ground flora stripped to a billiard-table sward — 'a canopy of pensioners with no heirs'
Rainforest neglect (rarer): bramble, holly and rhododendron grow up and smother the bryophytes/lichens, shading out seedlings
Rhododendron invasion; conifer underplanting; deadwood 'tidied' away; even-aged, no continuity
ecosystem_functions: The fullest energy flow and trophic complexity of any habitat in the series — full canopy light capture, deadwood, a food web to many trophic levels
Internationally important oceanic moss/liverwort/lichen/filmy-fern communities (the rainforest)
Soil-builder and carbon store — deep rooting, active litter cycling, carbon in soil and standing timber
High-value tree browse and mast (acorns) — the basis of historic wood-pasture/pannage
ecology.whole_system_role: The climax destination for most acid ground below the treeline and the flagship of the wilder end — the resolution of the grassland→scrub→woodland story.

successional_neighbours.note: Reached via pioneer/thorn scrub nursing tree regeneration on acid grassland — given low browsing.
Grades up to montane scrub and heath at the natural treeline.

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Upland Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Rainforest status, regeneration-as-diagnostic, browsing-not-planting, the rainforest neglect failure mode.

ukhab_condition.condition_grade_note: Assigned per parcel; the diagnostic is regeneration (is there a next generation of trees?).

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Favourable
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Full age range incl. regeneration and deadwood; rich ground flora and (west) bryophyte/lichen communities; native dominant; glades and graded edge.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable recovering
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Browsing reduced; regeneration beginning; rhododendron/conifer being removed.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable no change
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Mature canopy with sparse regeneration; some flora loss or invasive ingress.

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Unfavourable declining
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Overgrazed (no regeneration, stripped flora) OR neglected rainforest (bramble/holly/rhododendron smothering bryophytes).

condition_grade_descriptors.label: Destroyed / converted
condition_grade_descriptors.description: Rhododendron-choked, conifer-replaced, or canopy lost with no continuity.

sources.title: Woodland Condition Assessment (regeneration, age structure, browsing indicators)
sources.specific_claim: Regeneration potential (seedlings/saplings/young trees) and browsing pressure are core woodland condition indicators.

abiotic_template.parent_material_geology: Acid; brown earths and brown-podzolic soils on upland slopes
abiotic_template.climate: Spans the uplands; the rainforest form needs the wet, mild, oceanic west
abiotic_template.altitude_range_m: Valley floor up to the natural treeline
abiotic_template.aspect_exposure: The better, deeper, more sheltered ground the open habitats sit above
abiotic_template.bioclimate_notes: Rainforest: West Highlands & Islands and wetter western fringes (plus Welsh/SW fragments).

elemental_conditions.convention_note: CURRENT-STATE reading. Ancient/rainforest = Protect & Restore; new native oak on degraded ground = Regenerate→Bioregionally Wilder.

water.descriptor: Free-draining acid slopes; the rainforest form in the high-rainfall oceanic west.

earth.descriptor: Brown earths/brown-podzolics building under the canopy — deep rooting, active litter cycling, carbon stored in soil and timber. A soil-builder.

air.descriptor: The canopy creates its own humid, sheltered bioclimate — essential to the rainforest lower plants.

fire.descriptor: The fullest light capture and most complete energy flow of any habitat — canopy, deadwood, and a food web to many trophic levels.

successional_envelope.current_stage: Climax (the natural top of succession for acid ground below the treeline)
range_of_natural_variability: New native oak colonising degraded open ground
successional_envelope.successional_pathway: bare ground → grassland → scrub → oakwood (climax). NOT the right outcome on deep peat, above the treeline, or over open priority habitats. 'The right tree in the right place' — and the right place is most acid upland below the treeline, but emphatically not those exceptions.

wild_assessment.energy_flow_role: The fullest energy flow and trophic complexity in the series — the flagship of the wilder end.
wild_assessment.wild_zone_rationale: Ancient and semi-natural oakwood, and the temperate rainforest above all, are Protect & Restore — irreplaceable, internationally important, the seed sources for everything else. New native oak spreading across degraded open ground (via reduced browsing) is the core Regenerate→Bioregionally Wilder action. Per parcel: ancient/rainforest = P&R; new regeneration = Regenerate/Wilder.
wild_assessment.disturbance_regime: Browsing-controlled, not stocked. The primary lever is REDUCING deer/sheep browsing so trees regenerate. But managing a wood is not leaving it alone — in the rainforest a low (often winter) conservation-grazing level holds back smothering bramble/holly/rhododendron; too little grazing there is as damaging as too much.

wild_management_target.detail: Achieve and sustain natural regeneration by getting the browsing level right: low enough that seedlings survive, but (in rainforest) not so low that bramble/holly/rhododendron smother the bryophytes. Protect ancient/rainforest remnants; remove rhododendron and conifer; keep deadwood and veterans. Natural regeneration first, planting (native, local-provenance) only where the seed source is gone.
integrity_indicators: oceanic moss/liverwort/lichen/filmy-fern communities intact (the truest rainforest sign)
western oakwood bird trio (pied flycatcher, wood warbler, redstart)

wild_assessment.ecological_value_notes: Among the very highest — the Atlantic temperate rainforest is internationally important and effectively irreplaceable, with oceanic lower-plant communities found almost nowhere else in Europe. Even non-rainforest upland oak is high value and the climax flagship.

rarity_irreplaceability.notes: Temperate rainforest is globally rare and irreplaceable; ancient oakwood seed sources cannot be recreated.

diversity.notes: Internationally important lower-plant communities plus type-specific bird/invertebrate/deadwood assemblages.

energy_flow_contribution.notes: The fullest energy flow and trophic complexity in the series.

naturalistic_lu_ha.value: Near nil to very low — e.g. NRW suggests as little as ~1 sheep/ha over winter in some Atlantic oakwoods to hold back smothering growth
naturalistic_lu_ha.basis_note: A low, often winter, conservation-grazing level (sometimes cattle, sometimes light sheep) — NOT fence-and-forget. Pressures genuinely debated and site-specific. The dominant action is reducing deer/sheep browsing to allow regeneration.

wild_assessment.mosaic_role_note: The wooded climax the scrub mosaic leads to; provides browse and mast but its role is as the destination, not a grazing patch.
wild_assessment.preferential_grazing_magnet_note: Young trees ARE high-value browse — which is exactly why the wood cannot renew under heavy grazing; the palatability of seedlings is the mechanism that keeps the hill treeless.
wild_assessment.process_indicators_note: Regeneration is the master indicator; in rainforest, balance against smothering growth.

sources.title: Wilderculture field sheet 10 — Native Woodland
sources.specific_claim: Elemental reading, zone, browsing/regeneration management, rainforest grazing nuance.

forage.overall_palatability_notes: Off the top of the open-ground ladder — tree browse is high-value feed and the sheltered ground flora is good grazing. That is the whole tension: a wood is palatable, which is why under heavy grazing it cannot renew itself. Not managed as a forage resource; the aim is regeneration, not offtake.

forage_yield_by_grade.notes: NOT a forage/stocking habitat — managed by browsing level for regeneration, not DM offtake. See naturalistic_lu_ha for the low conservation-grazing figure.

forage_yield_literature.notes: Not a grazing resource; browse value drives the regeneration problem, not a yield budget.

forage.utilisation_factor_note: Not applicable — woodland is managed by browsing level, not DM × utilisation.

conservation_lu_ha.value: Near nil — the dominant action is REDUCING deer/sheep browsing to allow regeneration. Where conservation grazing is used (esp. rainforest), a low, often winter level — e.g. ~1 sheep/ha over winter (NRW, Atlantic oakwoods).
conservation_lu_ha.scheme: site-specific; debated — not a standard GLU/LU stocking figure
conservation_lu_ha.basis_note: Over 40% of Scotland's rainforest is grazed too heavily for any regeneration. The lever is browsing reduction (deer first in Scotland, sheep elsewhere), then a managed low level to hold back smothering growth without harming the lower plants. Genuinely debated and site-specific — verify with current restoration guidance.

sources.title: Woodland Trust / Alliance for Scotland's Rainforest — grazing and regeneration
sources.specific_claim: >40% of Scotland's rainforest grazed too heavily to regenerate; browsing reduction is the primary action.

sources.title: Natural Resources Wales Atlantic oakwood grazing guidance
sources.specific_claim: As little as ~1 sheep/ha over winter suggested in some Atlantic oakwoods to hold back smothering growth.

forage.palatability_rank: High (tree browse) — but a regeneration liability, not a grazing asset
forage.nutritional_quality_note: No significant toxicity theme; woodland is a soil-builder. Browse and mast are nutritious — the point is keeping browsing low enough to regenerate.

seasonal_forage_curve.spring: Browse and ground flora (a regeneration risk)
seasonal_forage_curve.winter: Low; the season for any light conservation grazing

optimal_grazing_animals.breed: Light cattle or low winter sheep (conservation grazing only)
optimal_grazing_animals.notes: In rainforest, a low (often winter) level holds back bramble/holly/rhododendron — NRW ~1 sheep/ha winter example. Otherwise the goal is reduced browsing, not stocking.

unsuitable_animals.reason: The single biggest threat to native woodland survival and expansion — they prevent regeneration.

timing_windows.preferred_season: Any conservation grazing usually winter; otherwise reduce year-round browsing
timing_windows.rationale: Allow regeneration to survive while holding back smothering growth in rainforest
timing_windows.per_species_notes: Deer management is the primary lever in Scotland; sheep elsewhere.

management.intensity_guidance: Reduce browsing first and foremost. Where conservation grazing is used, keep it low and often winter (see naturalistic_lu_ha) — managed low level, not fence-and-forget.
management.regulatory_note: Rhododendron: clear it and keep it out (the great invasive of western oakwoods). Plantation-on-ancient-woodland (PAWS): remove non-native conifer and restore the native wood beneath. Biosecurity underpins all of it. Disease/invasives are a live, moving area — verify current guidance; check designated-site consents (many rainforest sites are SSSI/SAC) before acting.
management.monitoring_protocol: Regeneration (seedlings/saplings/young trees), bryophyte/lichen community condition (rainforest), deadwood/veterans, rhododendron/conifer ingress, browse line. Apply the re-grade trigger.

bng_unit_value.value_note: Very high distinctiveness — priority habitat (H91A0 rainforest oak). Among the highest BNG values; effectively irreplaceable where ancient/rainforest.

sources.title: Statutory Biodiversity Metric
sources.specific_claim: Upland oakwood very high distinctiveness; confirm exact label.

key_positive_indicators: abundant oceanic mosses, liverworts, lichens and filmy ferns (the rainforest signature)
acid-oakwood ground flora — bilberry, wavy hair-grass, bluebell, moss carpets
deadwood and veteran trees; graded edge and glades
key_negative_indicators: no regeneration / browse line / billiard-table sward (overgrazed)
integrity_indicators_fauna: western oakwood bird trio — pied flycatcher, wood warbler, redstart
woodcock, tawny owl, woodpeckers; black grouse on the edge
saproxylic (deadwood) beetles and flies; woodland fritillaries in glades
bats; recovering pine marten (tips grey→red squirrel balance)
integrity_indicators_flora: the oceanic moss/liverwort/lichen/filmy-fern communities are of international importance — their presence is the truest sign a rainforest fragment is intact
species_list.note: Skeleton only — full species records and joins in Phase 2B. The oceanic lower-plant assemblage is the key integrity relationship to capture.

fields_fully_populated: ukhab_identity (w1a verified; Crosswalk corrected — oak is w1a, separate from birch w1e)
browsing/regeneration management and the rainforest grazing nuance
fields_partially_populated: naturalistic_lu_ha / conservation grazing — genuinely debated and site-specific (moderate confidence)
fields_pending_research: Current rhododendron/biosecurity guidance — verify before works
data_completeness.notes: First of five per-type woodland records. The flagship of Bioregionally Wilder and, in its rainforest form, an irreplaceable Protect & Restore jewel. The master management truth: getting woodland back is about REDUCING browsing so it regenerates, not planting — but rainforest needs a managed low grazing level, not abandonment.

sources.title: Woodland Trust / Alliance for Scotland's Rainforest
sources.specific_claim: Rainforest status, browsing as chief constraint.
